spec_match.rationale: Covers part of the spec (1 of 3 named API surfaces) — distinct angle worth picking up.

interactive.rationale: Interactive surface exists but is limited — more controls or a richer live readout would help.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Has 12 raw hex colour value(s) inline — should use --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens instead.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for element-scoped-view-transitions
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Element-scoped view transitions (CSS View Transitions 2). element.startViewTransition() on a subtree (must have layout containment). view-transition-group property (contain | nearest | <ident>) for nested groups. Concurrent transitions on different subtrees allowed. Lifecycle: pending-capture → update-callback-called → animating → done. Promises: updateCallbackDone, ready, finished. Edge cases: co...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: Add more controls / a richer live readout so the user can drive the feature through its full input space, not just a single canned example.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the design-system compliance
openQuestions.detail: Either replace raw hex with --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens, or pair every button background override with a color override (the global button rule sets both — overriding one alone risks light-on-light).

summary: Concept needs follow-up work before it really lands. 4 open question(s) queued.
